content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 7, 2017

Rachel Hundley
Mayor, City of Sonoma
1 The Plaza
Sonoma, CA 95476

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. W-17-019

Dear Mayor Hundley:

       This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice
to any person whose duties under the Act are in question. (Section 83114 (b); Regulation 18329(a).)
The Commission however may decline to provide advice where the advice would relate to past
conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Moreover, the Commission is statutorily prohibited from
rendering advice related to past conduct in connection with the conflict of interest provisions under
Government Code section 1090, et seq. (Section 1097.1(c)(2)).²

       After a careful review of the facts provided in your emails dated December 21 and
December 22, 2106, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. You would like to
know if the Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibits you from voting to renew a contract
between the Farmers Market and the City of Sonoma. It is plain from your emails however that you
have already participated in votes (December 12 and December 19, 2016) directly relating to the
Farmers Market. And you consulted with the City Attorney prior to voting. Any advice we provide
at this time would therefore relate to past conduct.

       If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                                              Sincerely,
                                                              Hyla P. Wagner
                                                              General Counsel

                                                              By: [signature]
                                                              Jack Woodside
                                                              Senior Counsel, Legal Division


¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, from making
contracts in which they are financially interested.

sections.question_synthetic: Mayor Hundley sought advice on whether the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit her from voting to renew a contract between the Farmers Market and the City of Sonoma.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the Mayor had already participated in votes on the matter (December 12 and 19, 2016) and consulted with the City Attorney beforehand, making any advice relate to past conduct, which the Commission is prohibited from addressing under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and Government Code section 1097.1(c)(2).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter (File No. W-17-019W). No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; information extracted from letter body. Minor apparent typo in document ('2106' likely means '2016'). Clear declination rationale based on prohibition against advising on past conduct.

QUESTION: Mayor Hundley sought advice on whether the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit her from voting to renew a contract between the Farmers Market and the City of Sonoma.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the Mayor had already participated in votes on the matter (December 12 and 19, 2016) and consulted with the City Attorney beforehand, making any advice relate to past conduct, which the Commission is prohibited from addressing under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and Government Code section 1097.1(c)(2).

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide advice to Mayor Rachel Hundley regarding whether she can vote on renewing a Farmers Market contract because she had already voted on related matters and the requested advice would address past conduct.
